body: More than 37% of the stocks in the S&P 500 rose during April. And of those, four stocks jumped more than 40% in just one month.
Indeed, some 90% of the index's rise is down to just 7 companies; Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla, and Alphabet. Obviously no one

In four months, four S&P 500 stocks turned $10,000 into $109,607. Intel led the gains in April. This outperformance indicates strong sector performance.

## How To Turn $10,000 Into $109,607 In 4 Months

| Month | Top S&P 500 stock | Symbol | Monthly gain | Sector | S&P 500 monthly ch. | Starting balance | Cumulative value of $10,000 investment in January reinvested in best stock each month |
 ---  ---  ---  --- |
| January | Sandisk | SNDK | 142.8% | Information Technology | 1.4% | $10,000 | $24,280 |
| February | Texas Pacific Land | TPL | 50.5% | Energy | -0.9% | $24,280 | $36,541 |
| March | LyondellBasell Industries | LYB | 40.1% | Materials | -5.0% | $36,541 | $51,195 |
| April | Intel | INTC | 114.1% | Information Technology | 10.4% | $51,194 | $109,607 |

##### Source: S&P Global Market Intelligence

   MATT KRANTZ
   Updated 07:42 AM ET 05/01/2026

April was a pleasant surprise for most S&P 500 investors. But some stocks did even better than the index.

If you invested $10,000 in January in the top-performing stock in the S&P 500 at the time and reinvested that in each month's top performer, including computer chip firm Intel (INTC) in April, you'd have $109,607 now, says an Investor's Business Daily analysis of data from S&P Global Market Intelligence and MarketSurge.

# 4 S&P 500 Stocks Turn $10,000 To $109,607 In Four Months

Image 3: FacebookImage 4: XImage 5: LinkedInImage 6: Share

Licensing

   MATT KRANTZ
   Updated 07:42 AM ET 05/01/2026

April was a pleasant surprise for most S&P 500 investors. But some stocks did even better than the index.

If you invested $10,000 in January in the top-performing stock in the S&P 500 at the time and reinvested that in each month's top performer, including computer chip firm Intel (INTC) in April, you'd have $109,607 now, says an Investor's Business Daily analysis of data from S&P Global Market Intelligence and MarketSurge.
